title: io_types: per-event UTC timestamp computation overhead
description:
Every BotEvent constructor calls utc_now() which invokes Utc::now() + to_rfc3339(). Under high relay throughput, this creates repeated syscalls and allocations.

Cost: One clock syscall per event. Measurable under load with dozens/second from broadcast messages.

Fix: Compute timestamp once at dispatch site, pass to constructors. Or cache timestamp with fine-grained updates.
close_reason: premature optimization: chrono::Utc::now() per event is negligible overhead for a bot
